message:
Security: Fix compound command quote bypass vulnerability

The previous quote-counting heuristic in NormalizeCommand incorrectly
treated commands like `echo "foo" && rm -rf /etc` as non-compound.
This was because it detected >= 2 quotes in the command and assumed
the compound separators (&&, ;, etc.) were inside quotes.

This vulnerability allowed dangerous commands to bypass detection when
hidden after a benign quoted command.

Fix: Replaced the naive quote count check with proper shell-aware
tokenization (splitCompoundShellAware) that respects single/double
quote state and escape sequences. Now:

- `echo "foo" && rm -rf /etc` correctly splits into two segments
- `psql -c "DELETE FROM users;"` correctly stays as one segment
- Nested quotes like `bash -c 'echo "x && y"'` are handled properly

Added comprehensive test coverage for the new splitting function
and security regression tests.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
